title: neea woronovii
intro: Neea woronovii, also known as the Red Jewel tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it is used for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Herbal preparations of Neea woronovii, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
